Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: src/rules.rs::validate_schema_and_fixtures + whetstone validate-rules CLI subcommand mirror the legacy Python contract. scripts/validate-rule-schema.py deleted. CI schema-check, pre-push hook, CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md all call the Rust binary. Rust tests cover pass-on-repo and fail-on-bad-fixture.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: scripts/detect-patterns.py deleted. tests/test_script_contracts.py TestPRMining + TestTranscriptPrivacy + the two detect-patterns next_command tests inside TestExhaustiveOutputContracts removed. ACTIVE_SCRIPTS_DIR constant removed. All detect-patterns coverage now lives in tests/rust_integration.rs. README/SKILL.md/AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md updated to drop Python helper references.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: schema-check and actionlint CI jobs no longer use actions/setup-python or pyyaml — shell + jq + awk + the Rust binary handle every check. python-test renamed to python-parity-tests with an explicit comment that the remaining Python jobs exist solely to lint and parity-test scripts/legacy/ reference implementations, which is the explicitly-justified exception called out in the acceptance criteria.

Shipped in commit 5aabb8f: install.sh gains a WHETSTONE_INSTALL_LOCAL_SRC escape hatch so CI can exercise the full install path without a tagged release. New install-smoke CI job runs install.sh, then runs --version/--help/status/detect-deps/ci-check/validate-rules from a directory outside the repo checkout against tests/fixtures. packaging/homebrew/whetstone.rb + packaging/homebrew/README.md provide a ready-to-publish Homebrew formula template (per-target URLs, sha256 placeholders, test do block) with maintainer publishing instructions. The actual angusbezzina/homebrew-tap repo is a one-time external action documented in packaging/homebrew/README.md.

Final cutover verification complete as of commit ce80078:
- install.sh (no repo checkout needed): verified locally via WHETSTONE_INSTALL_LOCAL_SRC and in the install-smoke CI job
- whetstone doctor/status/detect-deps/ci-check/validate-rules run from an external mktemp dir against tests/fixtures in install-smoke
- detect-patterns is fully Rust (scripts/detect-patterns.py deleted); subcommand pinned by tests/rust_integration.rs
- No Python is required for runtime or install: curl | sh + single binary; Python is ONLY present in CI as explicitly-justified parity tests against scripts/legacy/
- Docs (README, SKILL.md, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md), CI (schema-check, install-smoke, actionlint), GitHub Action (action.yml uses rust-toolchain + cargo build), and tests all match the Rust-first reality
- 'Python runtime' and 'neighboring checkout' no longer appear anywhere in docs/tests/workflows as a requirement

Acceptance criteria were already satisfied by the existing .githooks/pre-push + the ruff + push-time instructions in AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Updated this session: the pre-push hook now calls 'cargo run --release -- validate-rules' instead of the deleted Python validator, so the guardrail still matches CI exactly. Local ruff + cargo test + schema validation all pass from the hook.

Dogfooding complete. 6 rules across 4 deps (serde_yaml, anyhow, reqwest, clap). Key findings: (1) Content is null for all deps — no llms.txt, binary doesn't fetch HTML docs. Agent must bring own knowledge or fetch docs independently. (2) Status works perfectly — score 100, all dimensions correct. (3) Context generation works — Do/Don't sections, source URLs, examples. Only generates configured formats (default: agents.md). (4) Test generation produces scaffolds with TODO stubs — tests compile and pass but don't check anything. tree-sitter needed for real signal checks. (5) validate command only checks fixtures, not whetstone/rules/. (6) extract --show would be useless since content is null. The real gap is content fetching for non-llms.txt deps.
Validated during dogfooding. generate-context produces well-structured AGENTS.md with Do/Don't sections, source URLs, and examples. Works correctly with real rules.

Validated during dogfooding. generate-tests produces Rust test scaffolds that compile and pass. Tests are TODO stubs — real signal checks require tree-sitter (whetstone-esp). Python/TS smoke-test deferred to when real rules exist for those langs.

Proved unnecessary by dogfooding. Doctor output content is null for non-llms.txt deps — extract --show would return nothing. The agent uses its own knowledge or web search to extract rules. The real gap is content fetching, not content access.

Implemented 3-tier content fetching. Tier 1: registry READMEs (npm readme field, PyPI description, crates.io /readme endpoint). Tier 2: HTML fallback using scraper crate (extract main/article content, convert to structured text). Tier 3: docs_url_only (unchanged). Results: all 10 Whetstone deps now have content (previously all null). Zero new API calls for npm/PyPI (README already in response). One extra call for crates.io. HTML conversion extracts headings, code blocks, lists.
